title: Dimension Provider
description: Full provider definition for a dimension collection — describes how members are produced or served, applicable to any dimension type. A provider exposes capabilities as standard Records sub-resources: /items (paginated member listing, OGC Records Part 1), /inverse (value-to-member mapping; requires invertible:true), /search (query-based discovery), and the optional hierarchical pair /children + /ancestors (requires Hierarchical conformance level). Capabilities are declared via conformsTo URIs on the collection and via links.

type.description: Provider type identifier. Short identifiers name well-known algorithms maintained by the OGC Naming Authority; full URIs identify custom providers whose OpenAPI definition is given in the 'api' field. Well-known types: 'daily-period' (sub-monthly temporal periods configured by config.period_days and config.scheme — covers dekadal, pentadal, and any fixed-length day period), 'integer-range' (evenly-spaced integer bins), 'static-tree' (recursive in-memory hierarchy), 'leveled-tree' (leveled in-memory hierarchy with ?level=N filtering). This list is extensible: implementations MAY register additional well-known types through the OGC Naming Authority, and custom providers use a full URI as the type value.

api.description: OpenAPI definition URI. REQUIRED when type is a full URI (custom provider). For well-known types the API definition is implicit (resolvable from the registered OGC Definition URI). Machine clients fetch this to discover all available endpoints and parameters.

config.description: Author-set configuration values for this provider instance. These are static constants fixed at collection-authoring time and cannot be overridden by API clients per-request. They configure the provider algorithm itself — for example, the bin width of an integer-range provider or the base temperature of a growing-degree-day provider. For well-known provider types the set of recognised properties is defined by the OGC Definition URI for that type; for custom providers it is defined in the 'api' OpenAPI document. Well-known providers with no author-configurable constants (e.g. dekadal, pentadal) SHOULD omit this field or set it to {}. Clients that fetch the /dimensions list or /queryables endpoint will find these values in the response under 'config', enabling them to understand the exact parameterisation in use without reading server source code.

parameters.description: JSON Schema 2020-12 document describing the query-time parameters that clients may pass per request to /items, /children, and /search. These are NOT static configuration values — use the 'config' field for author-set constants. For hierarchical generators using a leveled strategy, individual hierarchy levels may supply their own parameters object containing the actual filter values (e.g. {"level": 0}) used to select members at that level — these are parameter instances, not schemas. Standard cross-generator parameters (SHOULD be declared here when the corresponding capability is present): 'language' (string, RFC 5646 Language-Tag — selects label language and sort collation; overrides Accept-Language header); 'sort_by' (string, output field name to sort members by, e.g. 'label', 'code', 'rank'; default is generator-defined); 'sort_dir' (string enum 'asc'|'desc', default 'asc'; used with sort_by).

output.description: Type and structure of each generated member, defined per JSON Schema 2020-12. For recursive hierarchical generators, the output object SHOULD include the field named in hierarchy.parent_property, typed as string or null (null for root members). Well-known optional output fields: 'label' (string, human-readable name in the default language), 'labels' (object, multilingual names keyed by RFC 5646 language tag — e.g. {"en": "France", "fr": "France", "ar": "فرنسا"} — when present, clients SHOULD use labels[negotiated_lang] over label), 'rank' (integer, explicit sort position for ordinal dimensions; 0-based), 'has_children' (boolean, true if this member has at least one child; saves a /children round-trip for tree rendering).

invertible.description: true if the generator supports inverse operations (Invertible conformance level). Exposes /inverse endpoint: maps a raw value back to the dimension member it belongs to. The forward function is a total surjection from domain values to members, partitioning the domain into disjoint intervals; /inverse computes the unique member containing a given value. Most useful for deterministic generators such as temporal calendars and integer ranges.

search.description: Supported search protocols (Searchable / Similarity conformance levels). 'exact': exact match on member code. 'range': members within bounds. 'like': pattern match (fnmatch-style). 'vector': k-nearest-neighbour search by embedding similarity.

on_invalid.description: Item ingestion behavior when inverse validation fails (invertible generators only). 'reject': return 422, block ingestion. 'accept': allow the item and grow the dimension. 'warn': allow the item and emit a warning. Ignored when invertible is false.

hierarchical.description: true if the provider supports Hierarchical conformance level: /children, /ancestors, and the optional ?parent= filter on /items. SHOULD be true when the dimension declares a hierarchy property. The provider's /children endpoint follows the same pagination contract as /items and mirrors the STAC API Children Extension (https://api.stacspec.org/v1.0.0-rc.2/children) applied to dimension members rather than collections.

navigable.description: true if the provider supports per-member navigation links (rel:children, rel:ancestors) when clients pass ?links=true. When navigable is true and ?links=true, each member in a /items or /children response MAY include navigation links. By default, member-level links are suppressed to minimize response size; response-level pagination links (self, next, prev) are always included. Requires hierarchical: true.

language_support.description: Languages the generator can serve member labels in. Each entry is a Language Object aligned with the STAC Language Extension (https://stac-extensions.github.io/language/v1.0.0/schema.json) and OGC API - Records. When present, clients MAY request a specific language via the 'language' query parameter (RFC 5646 Language-Tag, same format as Accept-Language) or the Accept-Language HTTP header. The generator SHOULD return the 'label' field in the negotiated language and SHOULD include a 'labels' map on each member with all available translations. Sort operations on 'label' use locale-aware collation for the negotiated language.

name.description: Name of the language in the language itself (e.g. 'Deutsch' for German). MUST NOT be translated.

alternate.description: Name of the language in another well-understood language, usually English (e.g. 'German' for German).

dir.description: Text direction for this language. 'ltr' (left-to-right) or 'rtl' (right-to-left). Clients rendering member labels SHOULD apply this direction. Defaults to 'ltr'.
